mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower recession risk, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid when the policy rate has declined from its prior 3-month level, because easing must be underway rather than already priced.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation deceleration improves real-rate expectations and supports equity multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when current inflation is below its recent trend, because disinflation reduces pressure for tighter policy.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change drops below the prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher 5d SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if inflation slowdowns are followed by negative returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and deteriorating earnings visibility, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional only when unemployment is still moving up from an already elevated base, because a stable high unemployment rate is less informative than a fresh deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20d returns are not negative on average after 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rises, or if the trigger condition has no measurable deterioration in forward return hit rate versus unconditional periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and broad cyclical demand, which should pressure equities over the next 60 trading days through earnings revisions. The effect fails when output weakness is isolated to one month and reverses immediately, because transient manufacturing noise does not persist long enough to affect forecasts.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX returns are not lower after 3-month INDPRO declines than after flat or rising INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer and nominal demand, which supports corporate revenues and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is clearest when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-month spike, because persistent demand improves earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month RSAFS rises are not followed by above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A housing upturn in HOUST improves cyclically sensitive growth expectations and supports equities over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires that housing starts are rising while unemployment is not accelerating, because housing strength with labor deterioration does not translate into durable earnings optimism.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST regimes do not produce higher 60d SPX returns when UNRATE is stable or falling, or if interaction terms add no incremental predictive power versus HOUST alone.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration that should lead S&P 500 lower over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth and margins. The signal is strongest when payroll losses occur together with an unemployment increase, because the joint move confirms a genuine labor shock rather than measurement noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase does not precede negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, the market discounts restrictive policy for longer and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative. The effect fails once inflation has clearly fallen, because then the same policy rate is interpreted as near the end of the tightening cycle rather than an active headwind.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation rate remains above its 2-year median
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate high-inflation periods do not show negative 20d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL alone.
